statement.en: Radiative Decoupling as a Topological Boundary Condition: A Geometric Normalization from Stellar Photospheres to the Cosmic Horizon
statement.summary: It is a operational reality of astrophysics that observers do not measure continuous 3D bulk volumes, but rather the 2D decoupling surfaces where radiation escapes. In this Letter, the operational requirement of radiative decoupling is formalized as a geometric boundary condition. By coupling measurable radiative observables with Newtonian surface gravity, a dimensionless boundary invariant, 𝒳, is defined. Evaluated analytically under standard macroscopic closures, 𝒳 reduces to an exact mass-independent identity. Observationally, using strictly independent solar measurements and a benchmark kinematic sample of 190 detached eclipsing binaries, stellar photospheres are found to lie below the theoretical blackbody phase-space capacity 𝒳₀ = π³/15 ≃ 2.0671. Projecting this strictly 2D surfac

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a novel theoretical framework (radiative decoupling as topological boundary condition) with analytical predictions (𝒳₀ = π³/15) tested against observational data (eclipsing binaries). The core claim—that stellar photospheres satisfy a mass-independent invariant—is empirically provisional: observations appear consistent with theory, but the sample is finite (N=190), the formalism is recent (2026), and the abstract cuts off mid-sentence, indicating incomplete validation. This is characteristic of preprint/early-stage work: mathematically coherent but not yet peer-reviewed or theoretically unified with established stellar physics frameworks.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 放射的脱結合は位相空間の幾何学的容量を支配する，普遍的な位相不変量として現れる。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 外層の2次元脱結合面は内部の3次元構造から光を解放する，位相的な境界として輝く。
